description:
A Hidden gem in a landmarked brownstone, right in the heart of the historic neighborhood of Bedford-Stuyvesant in Brooklyn.

With an abundance of local eateries and shops, you are bound to find something that tickles your fancy.

This suite is set in a prime location, perfect for those who commute both near & far. With access to the A, C & G trains, the LIRR and a multitude of bus lines you will have no problem exploring NYC and all it has to offer.
neighborhood_overview:
Bedford Stuyvesant, often referred to as "Bed-Stuy," is a historic neighborhood located in the central region of Brooklyn, New York City. The neighborhood's rich history and vibrant culture have made it a popular destination for tourists and residents alike. It's a lively and eclectic neighborhood that is full of life. It also has many fun and interesting things to get into. The neighbors are friendly and most importantly it's safe.

The architecture in Bedford Stuyvesant is predominantly made up of brownstone rowhouses, many of which were built in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. These elegant homes are known for their distinctive stoops and ornate ironwork, and many have been preserved or renovated to maintain their historic character.

name: Studio in the Heart of Chelsea Neighborhood
description: Spend your time in New York City relaxing in a chic studio in the heart of one of New York’s coolest neighborhoods. Look out at the tree-lined, busy streets from our boho apartment built in the 1800s, with all the history of old New York but renovated with modern upgrades. Chelsea Inn is a one-of-a-kind find in a vibrant, ever-evolving neighborhood. Home to over 350 art galleries, installations and projects, Chelsea boasts the bustling High Line Park built on an abandoned elevated railroad track
neighborhood_overview: Chelsea is a neighborhood on the west side of bustling Manhattan. It is a blend of historic apartment buildings and modern high-rises, with a ton of restaurants and boutiques dotting the streets. Chelsea is known for its many art galleries, which brings a whole creative wave of offerings with them. There are mom and pop pizza shops, as well as high-end, celebrity chef restaurants. It is the perfect neighborhood to bring out your inner foodie. You can stroll the streets of Union Square or the bohemian Greenwich Village, which are both walkable from your apartment, or catch the nearby Subway to anywhere in New York. We are lucky enough to be in one of the most accessible neighborhoods in all of NYC.
host_about: Chelsea is home to one of the most exciting art districts in the world, with over 350 art galleries, art installations and projects. Other must-sees are the famous Highline - a park built on an abandoned railroad track and the Chelsea Market, one of the world largest indoor urban markets. Chelsea has one of NYC's most varied food & drink scenes: from vegan fast food to high-end cocktails and everything in-between, it's all just steps away from our hotel doors.

name: Charming Boho Beach Retreat - walk to the ocean!
description:
We found a special place here in Long Beach & remodeled it to create a dream home. We still have some extra space so we want to share it with you! Our one bedroom, one bath guesthouse is full of ocean breezes, peace, and comfort to enjoy your stay in the LA/Orange County area.

We are former residents of West LA who wanted to move to a place with a little more chill, but still all of the things we love about LA. We found that here in LB. We know you'll appreciate this place as much as we do!
neighborhood_overview:
Historic Bluff Heights is tucked away on the southeast end of Long Beach. It is treelined and the homes are all nearly 100 years old (including ours!). It is artsy, hip, diverse, LGBTQ & family friendly (Shouldn't all hoods be like this though?) Laid back, friendly, and beachy vibe with coffee shops, restaurants, bars, and boutique shops, antique, & thrift stores all around. We are just 3 blocks to the beach and walking distance to the best of Broadway - including Steelhead coffeeshop, The Attic restaurant, The Firkin Pub, and Taste Beer and Wine bar - just to name a few. There is a local farmers market on Saturdays and Tuesday evenings as well as free yoga on the bluff overlooking the ocean nearby too.

While there is lots to do in Bluff Heights alone, you will also be walking distance to funky Retro Row, hipster Rose Park, happening Belmont Shore and also just a few miles to downtown Long Beach, where you can visit the Queen Mary, the Aquarium and Shoreline Village.
host_about:
We are LA locals who love to get out and explore the world, starting with our very own city. We've both lived here for over 10 years and have learned to love Los Angeles for all it has to offer. We're excited to share our home and our favorite places and things to do in this great city of Angels. 

We also love to get out of the city and explore new and visit old favorite corners of the country - as we like to quote the movie "Up": Adventure is out there!

description: Beautiful studio room with its own bathroom that faces the common courtyard of the building. Within walking distance to Times Square, Hell's Kitchen, Times Square /Broadway Theaters, and close to everything you might need! The perfect getaway in the middle of the city!
neighborhood_overview:
Time Square/Hell's Kitchen is an amazing neighborhood. The best of the city! This is the epicenter of NYC. Trains, buses and walk to anything you need within 5 minutes!

Hell's Kitchen, also known as Midtown West, is a neighborhood on the West Side of Midtown Manhattan in New York City. It is traditionally considered to be bordered by 34th Street to the south, 59th Street to the north, Eighth Avenue to the east, and the Hudson River to the west. Nestled at the base of the picturesque Griffith Park, it is known for its mix of charming historic homes, trendy shops, and a diverse food scene. The neighborhood offers a unique blend of urban living with a touch of nature, thanks to its proximity to one of the largest urban parks in North America.

Hollywood Blvd and Vermont Avenue serve as the main commercial corridors, featuring a range of boutique stores, cafes, restaurants, and bars. Popular spots include the Dresden Room, a classic cocktail lounge, and Little Dom’s, an Italian-American eatery.

neighborhood_overview: This furnished apartment is located in Burbank, a thriving city at the southeastern end of Los Angeles's Fernando Valley. This chic community located 12 miles northwest of Downtown LA has a spirit all its own. Burbank is the headquarters of both the Walt Disney and Warner Brothers studios, so creativity is a given here. Cool bars and cafes line West Burbank Boulevard, and the neighborhood is also home to one of LA's oddest attractions: the Bearded Lady's Mystic Museum. The diverse dining scene includes everything from American comfort food to Japanese to Thai. Anyone who loves to cook can fill up their grocery cart at Ralphs or Trader Joe's right in the area. Another local gem is the Urban Press Winery, which regularly hosts events and tastings on its lovely patio. While Burbank has its own train station, most residents rely on cars to get around.

We’re Blueground, a global proptech company with several thousand move-in-ready apartments in a growing number of major cities around the world. Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Even better? You can enhance your stay with car rentals, grocery delivery, laundry services, and more through our special partnerships. 

During your stay
Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

host_about:
Dear Airbnb guests!

Thanks for checking my profile. I manage furnished monthly rentals and co-living spaces in Manhattan, Queens, and Brooklyn with my team members. I have been managing furnished apartments for a decade and have had more than 5,600 guests from 90 countries so far. Please feel free to ask me any questions or requests!

*I can speak Japanese as well! Please feel free to contact me in Japanese.

Who we are: Our Principles

1) Growing Our Alumni Community:
We don’t require any extra/hidden fees to maximize our profits. Instead, our focus is on growing our alumni community. We believe that our services and fair pricing speak for themselves among our guests. Our alumni posted 700+ reviews from 2019 to 2021. What’s more, 80% of the reviews gave us five stars and 97% awarded more than four stars. Our alumni community spans the globe with 87 unique nationalities, and it has been expanding. We look forward to serving all our new guests: our alumni of tomorrow.
‍

‍2) Transparency. One All-Inclusive Price. That’s What We’re About:
There’s no math required. The price for our one-month minimum stay is simple and fair, no matter how long you stay with us. Our one-month price is already competitive (averaging $1,200 to $2,200, with 80% of our 150 rooms under $1,900 per month), compared to others’ discounted prices for three- to six-month stays. In 2020, 50%+ of our customers extended their original stay by a few months. There’s really no substitute for all-inclusive furnished rentals and a co-living environment, with a furnished private room, that’s ready for you on day one.
‍

‍3) Increasing Value for the Local Property Industry:
We provide co-living communities for our guests by helping property owners draw more value from under-managed residential properties. We care about our customer community, of course, but we’re also excited about uniting property owners, vendors, and our team on one digital platform.
‍

‍4) Privacy Is the Priority:
There are no required social events. Our priority is providing privacy, a quiet environment, and security for all our guests. Our team will take care of cleaning the common spaces every week, so that our kitchen/dining areas are always comfortable. But if you want to expand your network or social circle, our office at WeWork offers you day/after-work communities. We just moved our headquarters to the newest WeWork offices in Long Island City, which is easily accessible by all subway lines.


‍5) 10 Years Perfecting the Co-Living Mindset:
Our founder started this business in his apartment after he couldn’t find appropriately priced accommodation for his first three months in New York. But our whole team has experienced co-living firsthand, so we understand what the customer feels and will need next. “We live and breathe co-living: first as roommates, and now serving our co-living guests.”
‍

‍6) A Sustainable Business Model in the Sharing Economy:
We won’t invest in luxury buildings like hotels or event spaces. We rent existing empty apartments and townhouses from the owners, put IKEA-type furniture in, and list the clean rooms on multiple digital platforms. We grow by our operational profit base, which comes from guests being charged fair prices and the revenue owners receive. However, what drives our growth is not the number of rooms, but our deep understanding of how to care for guests and rooms 24/7, and the intangible value within them.
‍

‍7) Our Vision - Making Dreams Come True:
CrossOver’s goal is to help our guests achieve their goals and dreams by providing the most affordable and flexible housing environment available. But it doesn’t stop here. Our guests’ dreams are expanding to a variety of locations around the globe, and CrossOver will be right there with them—powering the dreams of residents, landlords, and our staff in the wider world.

You'll be staying at a place that is walking distance to enjoyable nightlife, dance clubs,  live music venues, comedy clubs, amazing restaurants, and cool local hangouts.  You're also a walk away from shopping on Robertson and Melrose, or the famous Sunset Strip and Sunset Plaza.    If you walk a few blocks south, you'll be at Urth Caffee which  it is known for its celebrity sightings.  You're also minutes away from Beverly Hills, The Grove, Farmers Market and CBS Studios!!  If you like the outdoors you are close to Runyon Canyon a great hiking spot. It's a fabulous neighborhood!!
host_about:
Hi everybody ! 
I’m a new renter here on Air BnB, and a native Southern Californian with an easy going and fun loving personality. As an ambitious, entrepreneurial and hardworking woman, owning my own company, I split my time between West Hollywood and Long Beach. I love hosting friends and family at my home, helping people, and spending time outside hiking, biking, or taking long walks by the beach. I’m health conscious and it’s a joy to prepare healthy food for my peeps! I play percussion and get a kick out of performing in drum circles, and I love to turn all my friends on to cool things that are happening in the city: concerts, events, gallery openings, etc. 
I’m grateful to live in a duplex where I can walk to all the cool spots in the heart of West Hollywood from Sunset Blvd. to Beverly Hills- and I don’t even have to get in my car! West Hollywood is the center of the entertainment industry, close to Runyon Canyon, and 20 min by car to beautiful beaches like Malibu and Santa Monica by way of Route 66- steps from my front door. I love my place and I’m always welcoming. I will go the extra mile to make sure my guests have a wonderful time visiting the best city in the world!

Upper East Side of Manhattan, New York City. It is considered a prime and upscale neighborhood known for its beautiful pre-war buildings, tree-lined streets, and proximity to some of the city's most notable landmarks and attractions. The area is home to many of the city's most prestigious museums, such as the Metropolitan Museum of Art, the Frick Collection, and the Whitney Museum of American Art.

East 78th St is also known for its high-end shopping and dining options. The neighborhood is home to many designer boutiques, luxury retailers, and high-end department stores such as Bergdorf Goodman and Saks Fifth Avenue. The area also has a diverse array of restaurants and bars, ranging from casual to fine dining options.

neighborhood_overview:
Did you know that besides being the birthplace of the famous Francis Ford Coppola, Woodside is also home to numerous restaurants and an easy commute route, residents can commute to Midtown on the 7 train in less than 15 minutes.

The area has long been home to a considerably large Irish, Italian and German populations. Over the years, however, the area is making a name for itself as a melting pot of cultures. Every year, Woodside is the host location of the “St. Pat’s for All Parade” welcoming members of the LGBTQ community to march every St. Patrick’s Day.

At the few blocks where 61st Street, Roosevelt Avenue, and Woodside Avenue meet, there are at least half a dozen pubs and restaurants.
The Alcove is one of the most famous gastropubs in the area. Nothing is better than a glass of red sangria. We recommend the Cuban sandwich with a side of fries.

New York is known as the crossroad of the world since it has every cuisine. Stop by Dawa’s for some
host_about:
Dear Airbnb guests!

Thanks for checking my profile. I manage furnished monthly rentals and co-living spaces in Manhattan, Queens, and Brooklyn with my team members. I have been managing furnished apartments for a decade and have had more than 5,600 guests from 90 countries so far. Please feel free to ask me any questions or requests!

*I can speak Japanese as well! Please feel free to contact me in Japanese.

Who we are: Our Principles

1) Growing Our Alumni Community:
We don’t require any extra/hidden fees to maximize our profits. Instead, our focus is on growing our alumni community. We believe that our services and fair pricing speak for themselves among our guests. Our alumni posted 700+ reviews from 2019 to 2021. What’s more, 80% of the reviews gave us five stars and 97% awarded more than four stars. Our alumni community spans the globe with 87 unique nationalities, and it has been expanding. We look forward to serving all our new guests: our alumni of tomorrow.
‍

‍2) Transparency. One All-Inclusive Price. That’s What We’re About:
There’s no math required. The price for our one-month minimum stay is simple and fair, no matter how long you stay with us. Our one-month price is already competitive (averaging $1,200 to $2,200, with 80% of our 150 rooms under $1,900 per month), compared to others’ discounted prices for three- to six-month stays. In 2020, 50%+ of our customers extended their original stay by a few months. There’s really no substitute for all-inclusive furnished rentals and a co-living environment, with a furnished private room, that’s ready for you on day one.
‍

‍3) Increasing Value for the Local Property Industry:
We provide co-living communities for our guests by helping property owners draw more value from under-managed residential properties. We care about our customer community, of course, but we’re also excited about uniting property owners, vendors, and our team on one digital platform.
‍

‍4) Privacy Is the Priority:
There are no required social events. Our priority is providing privacy, a quiet environment, and security for all our guests. Our team will take care of cleaning the common spaces every week, so that our kitchen/dining areas are always comfortable. But if you want to expand your network or social circle, our office at WeWork offers you day/after-work communities. We just moved our headquarters to the newest WeWork offices in Long Island City, which is easily accessible by all subway lines.


‍5) 10 Years Perfecting the Co-Living Mindset:
Our founder started this business in his apartment after he couldn’t find appropriately priced accommodation for his first three months in New York. But our whole team has experienced co-living firsthand, so we understand what the customer feels and will need next. “We live and breathe co-living: first as roommates, and now serving our co-living guests.”
‍

‍6) A Sustainable Business Model in the Sharing Economy:
We won’t invest in luxury buildings like hotels or event spaces. We rent existing empty apartments and townhouses from the owners, put IKEA-type furniture in, and list the clean rooms on multiple digital platforms. We grow by our operational profit base, which comes from guests being charged fair prices and the revenue owners receive. However, what drives our growth is not the number of rooms, but our deep understanding of how to care for guests and rooms 24/7, and the intangible value within them.
‍

‍7) Our Vision - Making Dreams Come True:
CrossOver’s goal is to help our guests achieve their goals and dreams by providing the most affordable and flexible housing environment available. But it doesn’t stop here. Our guests’ dreams are expanding to a variety of locations around the globe, and CrossOver will be right there with them—powering the dreams of residents, landlords, and our staff in the wider world.

name: Gorgeous Bed-Stuy duplex, 3bd + roof deck garden
description: On a tree-lined residential street in Bed-Stuy, you’ll find this lovely spacious home. The unit features a brand-new roof deck. As well as two large bedrooms (with king-size beds). Another bedroom with a bunk bed, (twin over full). In addition, three futons convert into beds, and there's a dedicated workspace. This home is also filled with great books, stunning plants, multiple private gardens, original art, and a 65” 4k smart tv. Two blocks from the A/C train (20 minutes from Manhattan).
neighborhood_overview:
Bedford Stuyvesant is the heart of Brooklyn. One of the things that makes it special is that the neighborhood is mostly residential. In the subsection Stuyvesant Heights there are plenty of amazing restaurants, cozy nightlife options, and areas to shop. As well as ample green spaces, and historic brownstones. There are no large buildings obstructing the views here, so from the roof you can see most of the NYC skyline.

Excellent restaurants and stores are all within walking distance. Saraghina might have the best pizza, Trad Room has the best sushi. Peaches and Peaches HotHouse are local favorites. Downtown’s classic older buildings and contemporary skyscrapers may not have the hype of other parts of Los Angeles, yet, but it’s quickly becoming the heart and soul of the city and is on it’s way towards reclaiming it’s status among all the glamor of Hollywood and its neighboring beachfront communities that make up the greater Los Angeles area. Below are some of our highlights of what can’t be missed while visiting DTLA.

neighborhood_overview:
Marina del Rey, often heralded as the "Jewel of Los Angeles," is an idyllic coastal enclave renowned for its serene harbors, luxurious waterfront homes, and a laid-back beachside ambiance. A haven for boaters and water sports enthusiasts, the world's largest man-made small craft harbor is the centerpiece of this community, dotted with sleek yachts and gentle sailboats.

The palm-fringed boulevards and Mediterranean-inspired architecture lend the neighborhood a resort-like feel. Here, residents and visitors alike can savor a delightful mix of fine dining restaurants, chic boutiques, and outdoor cafes that offer panoramic views of the glistening Pacific waters and mesmerizing sunsets. The promenade along the water is perfect for leisurely strolls, and the nearby Venice Beach Boardwalk injects an artistic and bohemian flair into the surroundings.

neighborhood_overview: The Rose Park South neighborhood in Long Beach has a rich history dating back to the early 1900s, with its first homes being Victorian-style. The cultural resurgence of downtown Los Angeles has been taking place for decades, but there’s no time like now to visit and experience DTLA which is on it’s way towards becoming the modern day metropolis that it was always destined to be. Downtown is filled with destinations for both locals and tourists, from museums and cultural institutions atop Bunker Hill, restaurants and bars in the Historic Core and sporting events in South Park. Downtown’s classic older buildings and contemporary skyscrapers may not have the hype of other parts of Los Angeles, yet, but it’s quickly becoming the heart and soul of the city and is on it’s way towards reclaiming it’s status among all the glamor of Hollywood and its neighboring beachfront communities that make up the greater Los Angeles area. Below are some of our highlights of what can’t be missed while visiting DTLA.

host_about:
Dear Airbnb guests!

Thanks for checking my profile. I manage furnished monthly rentals and co-living spaces in Manhattan, Queens, and Brooklyn with my team members. I have been managing furnished apartments for a decade and have had more than 5,600 guests from 90 countries so far. Please feel free to ask me any questions or requests!

*I can speak Japanese as well! Please feel free to contact me in Japanese.

Who we are: Our Principles

1) Growing Our Alumni Community:
We don’t require any extra/hidden fees to maximize our profits. Instead, our focus is on growing our alumni community. We believe that our services and fair pricing speak for themselves among our guests. Our alumni posted 700+ reviews from 2019 to 2021. What’s more, 80% of the reviews gave us five stars and 97% awarded more than four stars. Our alumni community spans the globe with 87 unique nationalities, and it has been expanding. We look forward to serving all our new guests: our alumni of tomorrow.
‍

‍2) Transparency. One All-Inclusive Price. That’s What We’re About:
There’s no math required. The price for our one-month minimum stay is simple and fair, no matter how long you stay with us. Our one-month price is already competitive (averaging $1,200 to $2,200, with 80% of our 150 rooms under $1,900 per month), compared to others’ discounted prices for three- to six-month stays. In 2020, 50%+ of our customers extended their original stay by a few months. There’s really no substitute for all-inclusive furnished rentals and a co-living environment, with a furnished private room, that’s ready for you on day one.
‍

‍3) Increasing Value for the Local Property Industry:
We provide co-living communities for our guests by helping property owners draw more value from under-managed residential properties. We care about our customer community, of course, but we’re also excited about uniting property owners, vendors, and our team on one digital platform.
‍

‍4) Privacy Is the Priority:
There are no required social events. Our priority is providing privacy, a quiet environment, and security for all our guests. Our team will take care of cleaning the common spaces every week, so that our kitchen/dining areas are always comfortable. But if you want to expand your network or social circle, our office at WeWork offers you day/after-work communities. We just moved our headquarters to the newest WeWork offices in Long Island City, which is easily accessible by all subway lines.


‍5) 10 Years Perfecting the Co-Living Mindset:
Our founder started this business in his apartment after he couldn’t find appropriately priced accommodation for his first three months in New York. But our whole team has experienced co-living firsthand, so we understand what the customer feels and will need next. “We live and breathe co-living: first as roommates, and now serving our co-living guests.”
‍

‍6) A Sustainable Business Model in the Sharing Economy:
We won’t invest in luxury buildings like hotels or event spaces. We rent existing empty apartments and townhouses from the owners, put IKEA-type furniture in, and list the clean rooms on multiple digital platforms. We grow by our operational profit base, which comes from guests being charged fair prices and the revenue owners receive. However, what drives our growth is not the number of rooms, but our deep understanding of how to care for guests and rooms 24/7, and the intangible value within them.
‍

‍7) Our Vision - Making Dreams Come True:
CrossOver’s goal is to help our guests achieve their goals and dreams by providing the most affordable and flexible housing environment available. But it doesn’t stop here. Our guests’ dreams are expanding to a variety of locations around the globe, and CrossOver will be right there with them—powering the dreams of residents, landlords, and our staff in the wider world.

name: Private room in charming, eclectic Flushing home
description: Charming, quirky, eclectic apartment furnished with memorable antiques. Modern, spacious kitchen. Large bedroom with full size bed featuring pillow top mattress and large down comforter, closet and dressers, desk/vanity, full-length mirror, carpeting, bookshelf, nightstand. Living room has grand piano from the 1960s and beautiful marble table. Hardwood floors.
neighborhood_overview: Flushing, Queens, New York is America's largest Chinatown. It features restaurants and shops reflecting the composition of its residents: Chinese, Korean, and Indian. Around the corner is a restaurant that is one of the few places in the United States that serves cuisine from the Dong Bei province of China. Also nearby is Korean barbecue restaurant visited by Anthony Bourdain for a recent episode of his television show. Around the corner is the busiest street in the neighborhood, a densely populated thoroughfare that features a plethora of Chinese bakeries, traditional medicine/herbs shops, bubble tea shops, and food carts. Citifield is extremely close by. Queens botanical garden is a quick walk away.
host_about: I am a recent law school graduate and will be living in London from August 15 to September 29. You'll be staying in my room, which is the master bedroom. My roommate will be your host during your stay; he's an amazing chef and can cook a diverse array of dishes. We have an extremely cozy, shared living room with beautiful furniture that belonged to my grandmother, who collected antiques. My favorite items in the living room are the marble table, embroidered antique love seat from the 1950s, ornate porcelain lamps that look like people, and of course, the beautiful (and soon to be tuned) grand piano. My roommate and I have scoped out all the best restaurants in the area (and there are many!) and would be happy to take you on a tour. My grandparents lived in this apartment and I have saved and maintained most of the original furnishings in the living room. The apartment has a lot of character and personality, and we are happy to tell you about its history (there are some pretty funny stories). My roommate and I love to spend lazy weekends chilling in the living room listening to Khia, eating delicious takeout and enjoying the ambiance.

name: Hollywood Hills | Prime Location & Views
description: SUPERHOST. Villa Hollywood is designed for outdoor living, featuring a terrace with stunning views. This 1932 Spanish Revival home blends global decor for a unique charm. Just a 5-minute walk to Hollywood Boulevard, the Walk of Fame & The Hollywood Bowl. Universal Studios & West Hollywood are a short drive away. Even Santa Monica is reachable in 30 minutes. neighborhood_overview: The canyons and slopes of the Hollywood Hills, which run from Hollywood west to the canyons above Beverly Hills, are best seen from the winding concourse of Mulholland Drive, threading the crest of the mountains. With its striking panorama after dark of the illuminated city-grid stretching nearly to the horizon, the road is a prime axis for the LA good life, with mansions so commonplace that only the half-dozen fully blown castles really stand out. For a more up-close look at landmark architecture, take in a concert at the Hollywood Bowl, the massive concrete bandshell whose summer music offerings tend toward the crowd-pleasing variety. Throughout Hollywood you can see the Hollywood Sign, erected as a property advertisement in 1923 (it actually spelled “Hollywoodland” until 1949) and illuminated with four thousand light bulbs. Nowadays, infrared cameras and radar-activated zoom lenses have been installed to catch graffiti writers.The best views can be had from the Griffith Observatory.
host_about: Superhost for 13+ years, I take pride in making guests feel at home. Originally from Canada and now based in LA, I’ve traveled all 7 continents and 50+ countries. Hosting guests from around the world is a joy, and I love sharing local tips tailored to their interests. When I’m not exploring, I welcome the world into my home!

A TRUE DESTINATION AMONG TIMES SQUARE HOTELS

Our location puts all that makes New York amazing at your fingertips. Visit off-the-beaten-path restaurants in Hell’s Kitchen, stroll through Central Park, or hop on one of the nearby subway lines and visit Brooklyn or renowned museums. It’s easy from here, and our concierge can help ease the way.

neighborhood_overview:
The Lower East Side has a reputation for being ahead of the curve. It's referred to the area alongside the East River from about the Manhattan Bridge and Canal Street up to 14th Street and roughly bounded on the west by Broadway. It included areas known today as East Village, Alphabet City, Chinatown, Bowery, Little Italy, and NoLIta.

This vibrant downtown neighborhood is a popular nightlife destination—its streets are lined with trendy places to drink, dance, and hear live music. Lively at night, conveniently located, and filled with beautiful community gardens, world-renowned restaurants, and hip bars.

This apartment is located in the newly minted "Dimes Square". Artists and celebrities like Hugh Jackman, Zoe Kravitz, and Taylor Swift are frequently spotted here.

With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer.

From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Why stress over your apartment? We provide a hassle-free alternative — a consistent, quality guest experience that starts even before you arrive. Because we let you book our most up-to-date apartment listings online, confirm with a click, pay securely, and check in easily. 

Even better? You can enhance your stay with car rentals, grocery delivery, laundry services, and more through our special partnerships. 

During your stay
Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

neighborhood_overview: Crown Heights is the place in Brooklyn where West Indians and other African Americans, Hassidic Jews, Gentrifiers, Africans and new Hispanic immigrants mash together near the Brooklyn Museum and the Grand Army Plaza. Known for its annual West Indian American Day Parade, which attracts nearly 4 million people every Labor Day weekend, the area has attracted loads of attention lately as a cipher for the gentrification washing over Brooklyn.

name: Downtown Culver 1 bedroom- Comfort and Convenience
description: This gorgeous unit is strong on comfort, style, and convenience.   Unwind in a deep down sofa and enjoy the large smart tv loaded with Netflix, Hulu and more. Put our full kitchen to work if you love to cook, or order takeout from any of the outstanding nearby restaurants. neighborhood_overview: Jackson is one of the older streets in the city, close to the heart of downtown and a half block away from Sony Picture Studios.  Many of the residents over the years have worked directly with the studios and in the industry as writers, makeup artists, set designers, editors, and more, and many of them find their way to Jackson Market.  Sony was first built as Culver Studios in 1915 then later sold to Samuel Goldwyn and formed into the MGM Studios in 1924, which went on to create many of Hollywood's most famous of classic movies including Gone With the Wind, The Wizard of Oz, and more. Jackson Market was built just a year later in 1925 and has been servicing the neighborhood, and the studios ever since.  Henry Culver built his beautiful triangle hotel just a few blocks away where he centered the heart of Culver City.  Today the hotel anchors a district full of restaurants, cafes, theaters, and bars all within a 5 minute walk.

Santa Monica Pier, with its ferris wheel and rides, is about a mile up the path to the north and Washington Square, with it's restaurants and outdoor cafe's, is within a few blocks to the south. Abbott Kinney Blvd, with some of the best dining and art galleries in LA, is just a short walk inland. There are great farmers markets close-by on Fridays and Sundays. Sydney and I recently tried Marina Parasailing and had a blast. It's a beautiful boat ride in the bay, and from 500-800 feet high you can see the whole of LA and her coastline. There are free Twilight Concerts every summer Thursday on the beach just south of the Santa Monica pier. Every Sunday afterno

- Soho is filled with boutiques, fancy chain stores, and high-end art galleries, a top shopping destination, especially for out-of-towners.
- Little Italy is one of the most iconic neighborhoods of NYC and offers a taste of New York Italian specialties hard to find anywhere else.
- Chinatown is a densely populated neighborhood that draws foodies and tourists to its many Chinese and Southeast Asian restaurants. The busy sidewalks are packed with souvenir stores, bubble tea shops, and markets selling everything from fresh and dried fish to herbs and spices.

description:

neighborhood_overview:
Established in the 20th century, Sunnyside retains a heavy Irish influence accompanied with courtyards and tree-lined streets. Just what New York is known for, the harmonious neighborhood in Queens also boasts global residents from Japan, Mexico, Romania, and many more.

Just six stops from Manhattan, you can find an incredibly dynamic food and social scene. Not to mention, Sunnyside is only a couple of stops away from Astoria should you want to take a weekend stroll.

If you are in the mood for some dishes from the land of the rising sun, Takesushi is the place to be. While the price tag may seem high for a Queens restaurant citizen, when compared with other places in Manhattan, especially when you consider the quality of the cuts, Takesushi is definitely offering a bargain. Our recommendation is the Kaisendon filled with blue-fin tuna, salmon and more over rice. If sushi is not your cup of tea, how about I Love Paraguay (haha, obvious much?) for empanadas and some
host_about:
Dear Airbnb guests!

Thanks for checking my profile. I manage furnished monthly rentals and co-living spaces in Manhattan, Queens, and Brooklyn with my team members. I have been managing furnished apartments for a decade and have had more than 5,600 guests from 90 countries so far. Please feel free to ask me any questions or requests!

*I can speak Japanese as well! Please feel free to contact me in Japanese.

Who we are: Our Principles

1) Growing Our Alumni Community:
We don’t require any extra/hidden fees to maximize our profits. Instead, our focus is on growing our alumni community. We believe that our services and fair pricing speak for themselves among our guests. Our alumni posted 700+ reviews from 2019 to 2021. What’s more, 80% of the reviews gave us five stars and 97% awarded more than four stars. Our alumni community spans the globe with 87 unique nationalities, and it has been expanding. We look forward to serving all our new guests: our alumni of tomorrow.
‍

‍2) Transparency. One All-Inclusive Price. That’s What We’re About:
There’s no math required. The price for our one-month minimum stay is simple and fair, no matter how long you stay with us. Our one-month price is already competitive (averaging $1,200 to $2,200, with 80% of our 150 rooms under $1,900 per month), compared to others’ discounted prices for three- to six-month stays. In 2020, 50%+ of our customers extended their original stay by a few months. There’s really no substitute for all-inclusive furnished rentals and a co-living environment, with a furnished private room, that’s ready for you on day one.
‍

‍3) Increasing Value for the Local Property Industry:
We provide co-living communities for our guests by helping property owners draw more value from under-managed residential properties. We care about our customer community, of course, but we’re also excited about uniting property owners, vendors, and our team on one digital platform.
‍

‍4) Privacy Is the Priority:
There are no required social events. Our priority is providing privacy, a quiet environment, and security for all our guests. Our team will take care of cleaning the common spaces every week, so that our kitchen/dining areas are always comfortable. But if you want to expand your network or social circle, our office at WeWork offers you day/after-work communities. We just moved our headquarters to the newest WeWork offices in Long Island City, which is easily accessible by all subway lines.


‍5) 10 Years Perfecting the Co-Living Mindset:
Our founder started this business in his apartment after he couldn’t find appropriately priced accommodation for his first three months in New York. But our whole team has experienced co-living firsthand, so we understand what the customer feels and will need next. “We live and breathe co-living: first as roommates, and now serving our co-living guests.”
‍

‍6) A Sustainable Business Model in the Sharing Economy:
We won’t invest in luxury buildings like hotels or event spaces. We rent existing empty apartments and townhouses from the owners, put IKEA-type furniture in, and list the clean rooms on multiple digital platforms. We grow by our operational profit base, which comes from guests being charged fair prices and the revenue owners receive. However, what drives our growth is not the number of rooms, but our deep understanding of how to care for guests and rooms 24/7, and the intangible value within them.
‍

‍7) Our Vision - Making Dreams Come True:
CrossOver’s goal is to help our guests achieve their goals and dreams by providing the most affordable and flexible housing environment available. But it doesn’t stop here. Our guests’ dreams are expanding to a variety of locations around the globe, and CrossOver will be right there with them—powering the dreams of residents, landlords, and our staff in the wider world.

- Little Italy is one of the most iconic neighborhoods of NYC and offers a taste of New York Italian specialties hard to find anywhere else.
